summary: CI failed because warnings were elevated to errors during the run, causing the job to exit with code 1.
root_cause: SKLEARN_WARNINGS_AS_ERRORS was set (SKLEARN_WARNINGS_AS_ERRORS: 1) which caused compiler/runtime warnings (from C-extension files) to be treated as errors and abort the test run.

evidence.reason: Shows the run reported multiple errors rather than only warnings.
Explicit environment/configuration setting indicating warnings are treated as errors.
A warning originating from a C-extension source file that would be promoted to an error under SKLEARN_WARNINGS_AS_ERRORS=1.
Another C-extension source file showing warnings present during the build/test run.
Indicates subprocess failures are configured to raise errors, contributing to the job abort.
The job terminated with a non-zero exit code, consistent with errors being raised from warnings.

suggested_fix: Either unset or disable SKLEARN_WARNINGS_AS_ERRORS in the CI environment to allow warnings, or fix the underlying warnings in the listed C-extension sources (e.g., sklearn/neighbors/_binary_tree.pxi and sklearn/_loss/_loss.c) and re-run the tests.
